root_text.sn24.19:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati, kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.19:1.3: ‘na vātā vāyanti, na najjo sandanti, na gabbhiniyo vijāyanti, na candimasūriyā udenti vā apenti vā, esikaṭṭhāyiṭṭhitā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.19:2.1: “Rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.19:2.6: viññāṇe sati, viññāṇaṁ upādāya, viññāṇaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.19:3.4: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya—
root_text.sn24.19:3.7: “Iti kho, bhikkhave, dukkhe sati, dukkhaṁ upādāya, dukkhaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.19:3.14: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya
root_text.sn24.19:3.17: “Iti kho, bhikkhave, dukkhe sati, dukkhaṁ upādāya, dukkhaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.19:3.18: ‘na vātā vāyanti, na najjo sandanti, na gabbhiniyo vijāyanti, na candimasūriyā udenti vā apenti vā, esikaṭṭhāyiṭṭhitā’”ti.

translation_text.sn24.19:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what, does the view arise:
translation_text.sn24.19:1.3: ‘Winds don’t blow; rivers don’t flow; pregnant women don’t give birth; the moon and stars neither rise nor set, but stand firm like a pillar’?”
translation_text.sn24.19:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn24.19:2.1: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.19:2.2: ‘Winds don’t blow; rivers don’t flow; pregnant women don’t give birth; the moon and stars neither rise nor set, but stand firm like a pillar.’
translation_text.sn24.19:2.6: consciousness exists, because of grasping consciousness and insisting on consciousness, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.19:2.7: ‘Winds don’t blow; rivers don’t flow; pregnant women don’t give birth; the moon and stars neither rise nor set, but stand firm like a pillar.’
translation_text.sn24.19:3.4: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would the view arise:
translation_text.sn24.19:3.5: ‘Winds don’t blow; rivers don’t flow; pregnant women don’t give birth; the moon and stars neither rise nor set, but stand firm like a pillar’?”
translation_text.sn24.19:3.7: “And so, when suffering exists, because of grasping suffering and insisting on suffering, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.19:3.8: ‘Winds don’t blow; rivers don’t flow; pregnant women don’t give birth; the moon and stars neither rise nor set, but stand firm like a pillar.’
translation_text.sn24.19:3.14: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would such a view arise?”
translation_text.sn24.19:3.17: “And so, when suffering exists, because of grasping suffering and insisting on suffering, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.19:3.18: ‘Winds don’t blow; rivers don’t flow; pregnant women don’t give birth; the moon and stars neither rise nor set, but stand firm like a pillar.’”
